description: When creating or editing a bead, allow the user to explicitly specify a parent issue via a search/select field in the form. Currently parent is only set implicitly via context (right-click on an issue). Explicit parent selection means the new issue ID will reflect the hierarchy from the start, removing the need to move it later.\n\nThe create form already accepts parentId via view state — this adds a UI picker so the user can set or change it manually.
title: Parent selector field in create/edit form

description: Replace the plain textarea for the description field with an Obsidian-native markdown editor that renders markdown during editing (similar to how native Obsidian notes work — live preview mode).\n\nInvestigate Obsidian API options: MarkdownRenderer, Editor component, or embedding a MarkdownView. The goal is WYSIWYG/live-preview editing, not just syntax highlighting.

description:
⭐ Prompt: Design the Perfect Obsidian-Style “View Bead” Screen

Context:
Design the View Bead screen for an Obsidian plugin integrating the Beads project (https://github.com/steveyegge/beads
).
This is a read-only view and must feel fully native to Obsidian.

🎨 Overall Design Goals

The screen should feel like reading a well-formatted Obsidian note, not a form.

Clean typography, excellent spacing, minimal chrome.

Native Obsidian visual language: dark/light support, tag pills, metadata blocks.

Information hierarchy:
Title → Metadata → Description → Notes → Dependencies

🧩 Layout Specification (Obsidian-Native)
Header

Back arrow/link: ← Back to beads

Large H1 title (bead title)

Optional breadcrumb line under the title

Top-right actions:

Primary button: Edit Bead

Overflow menu: ⋯ with Delete, Duplicate, etc.

Metadata Bar

A single horizontal metadata container styled like Obsidian’s “Properties” panel.

Include:

Type (with colored icon badge; use Obsidian semantic colors)

Priority

Assignee (avatar + name)

Labels (Obsidian-style tag pills)

Created / Updated timestamps (right-aligned, low-emphasis)

Visual rules:

Use Obsidian’s subtle panel background

Light padding like frontmatter/metadata blocks

No thick borders—optional faint separators only

Description Section

Render the bead’s description using Obsidian’s built-in markdown renderer. Support:

Headings

Lists

Blockquotes

Code blocks

Tables

Inline code

Everything should follow Obsidian’s reading mode typography exactly.

This is the primary content.

Notes Section (Optional)

Shown only if notes exist.

Use a callout-like container:

> [!note]
> Notes
> <notes content rendered as markdown>

Slightly rounded corners

Matches Obsidian callout styling

Dependencies Section

Two lists presented using Obsidian link/list conventions.

Depends on

A list of bead links representing prerequisites.

Each bead entry includes:

[[Bead Name]]

Type icon

Optional status or small metadata chip

Dependents

A list of beads that depend on this bead.

Same styling rules.

Bead rows should feel like Obsidian list items or minimal link cards.

🪶 Tone & Behavior

The entire UI should feel like part of Obsidian:

Avoid heavy custom UI or app-like components

Do not use card dashboards unless extremely subtle

Follow standard Obsidian spacing and typography

Use Obsidian theming variables (--background-primary, etc.)

Prioritize reading flow, not density

🚀 Output Format Expectations

The model should output:

A clear UI layout description

A component-by-component breakdown

Optional HTML-like structure reflecting Obsidian conventions

Optional CSS (using Obsidian variables) if needed
title: UI for view screen

description: The Create and Edit Bead forms should use the same visual language as the View Bead screen: document-style header with back button, inline-title-sized heading, borderless bottom-border title input, matching section label style (h3 + border-bottom), consistent container width (min(820px,100%)) and padding. This was already implemented in the previous session.
title: Unify Create/Edit form layout with View Bead screen

description: The Edit Bead form shows dependencies read-only. It should have the same interactive dep selector as the Create form: pre-populated with existing deps as removable chips, ability to add new deps via search dropdown, and on save diff old vs new deps to run bd dep add / bd dep remove.
title: Add dependency editing to Edit Bead form
Cover all CLI options

description: In panel user can have Beads from multiple projects. So we need a button to add a new project. When user ads a new project we display in the panel directory as the root.
title: User can add a new project to Beads

description: The CLI supports 'bd reopen <id> --reason' but the plugin has no way to reopen a closed issue. Add a Reopen action to the View Bead screen (in the More Actions menu) that calls bd reopen and optionally prompts for a reason. Currently only bd close is available.
title: Reopen closed issues

description: The CLI supports 'bd close <id> --reason' but the plugin's Close action passes no reason. Add an inline reason input when closing a bead — either a small modal/popover or an inline text field — so the reason is recorded in the audit trail.
design: When user clicks close show inline reason.
title: Close bead with reason

description: The CLI supports a 'deferred' status (put on ice) but the Edit Bead form STATUSES map only has open, in_progress, blocked, closed. Add 'deferred' to the status dropdown so users can defer issues through the UI.
title: Add Deferred status to edit form

description: The CLI supports --external-ref on both create and update (e.g. 'gh-123', 'jira-PROJ-456', or a full GitHub URL). Add an External Ref field to the Create and Edit Bead forms, and display it in the View Bead screen's metadata card. Use 'bd update <id> --external-ref' on save.
title: External reference field in Create/Edit forms

description: The CLI supports extensive filtering: --status, --type, --priority, --assignee, --label, --label-any, --title-contains, --no-assignee, --priority-min/max, date ranges, etc. The sidebar currently lists all issues. Add a filter bar or filter controls to the sidebar so users can narrow the issue list by status, type, priority, or text search.
title: Sidebar issue filtering and search

description: The CLI has 'bd ready' (issues with no blockers, not claimed) and 'bd stale --days N' (issues not updated recently). Add these as filter presets or dedicated sections in the sidebar so users can quickly find actionable work or issues that need attention.
title: Ready and Stale issue views

description: Add design notes fields when viewing issue.
title: In the view modes I can see design / notes fields

description:
Add the following fields to the View Bead metadata card, displayed when non-empty:
- due_at (Due date)
- close_reason (shown when issue is closed)
- closed_at (shown when issue is closed)
- created_by (who created the issue)
- owner (owner, distinct from assignee)

Show bd version in settings under the field to input path

close_reason: Implemented full UI redesign per detailed wireframe spec v2
description:
```
Redesign the Create/Edit Bead UI using this wireframe and behavior spec:

Goal:
- Cleaner layout
- Faster scanning
- Reduced vertical sprawl
- Logical grouping of related fields
- Better hierarchy of information

Follow this structure:

1. Title field (full width)

2. Metadata row (3 columns):
   - Type (dropdown)
   - Priority (dropdown)
   - Assignee (user search input)

3. Labels section:
   - Render existing labels as pills
   - Provide a "+ add label" entry

4. Parent selector:
   - Show current parent as a pill with remove (x)
   - Include search field for selecting a new parent

5. Description section:
   - Large markdown editor, 12–16 lines tall
   - Full width

6. Notes (optional):
   - Smaller textarea, 4–6 lines tall

7. Dependencies section:
   Subsections:
     - Blocks
     - Related
     - Discovered From
     - Tracks
   Each should display existing items as pills
   Each has a "+ add" control

8. Action bar:
   - Cancel (left)
   - Create/Update Bead primary button (right)

Styling guidelines:
- Use consistent spacing between sections
- Group related fields visually with subtle dividers or spacing
- Avoid long empty inputs—only expand sections when needed (e.g., dependencies)
- Use pill-style UI for labels and dependencies

Use this ASCII wireframe as reference:

+---------------------------------------------------------------+
|  Create / Edit Bead                                           |
+---------------------------------------------------------------+
Title: [_____________________________________________________]

Row: Type / Priority / Assignee
--------------------------------------------------------
 Type:     [ Task ▼ ]      Priority: [ P2 — Medium ▼ ]
 Assignee: [ user search... ]

Labels:
[ ui ][ backend ][ infra ][ + add label ]

Parent:
[ obsidian-beads-8 (x) ] [ search parent... ]

---------------------------------------------------------------
 Description
---------------------------------------------------------------
| [ Markdown editor — full width ]                             |
---------------------------------------------------------------

Notes (optional)
---------------------------------------------------------------
| [ smaller notes text area ]                                  |
---------------------------------------------------------------

Dependencies
---------------------------------------------------------------
 Blocks:         [ + add block ]
 Related:        [ + add related ]
 Discovered from:[ + add origin ]
 Tracks:         [ + add track ]

---------------------------------------------------------------
[ Cancel ]                                      [ Create Bead ]
---------------------------------------------------------------

Implement functionality and styling according to this spec.
```
title: Layout of edit and create bead

close_reason: Issues now render hierarchically using parent_id. Root issues show at top level, children nested underneath with expand/collapse. Epics/features/tasks show a '+' button to create child issues with --parent flag. Type badges shown for epics and features.
description: Please find out in beads docs the way to create hierarchy of issues. Like epic -> task etc. In our panel tree allow to render this and also put the + signs next to the element which can have child items.
title: Support sub-items

description:
When importing issues from JSONL, dependencies that lack a created_at field get Go zero value time.Time{} (year 0001). The MySQL driver writes this as 0000-00-00 00:00:00. When reading back with parseTime=true, the result is a time.Time with year=0, which fails time.Time.MarshalJSON with 'year outside of range [0,9999]', making bd list --json fail entirely.

Root cause: CreateIssuesWithFullOptions in internal/storage/dolt/issues.go inserts dep.CreatedAt directly without checking if it is zero.

Fix: before inserting, check if dep.CreatedAt.IsZero() and fall back to time.Now().UTC().

Workaround applied: UPDATE dependencies SET created_at = '2026-02-20 18:58:40' WHERE created_at = '0000-00-00 00:00:00' on beads_obsidian DB.
title: Bug: dependency created_at zero value causes JSON marshal failure after JSONL import

close_reason: Implemented: added created_at to BeadIssue type and updated sort to use it, falling back to trailing ID number
description: Currently the issue tree in the sidebar sorts by trailing numeric ID. It should sort by created_at descending so newest issues appear at the top, matching the order returned by bd list.
title: Sort issue tree by created_at descending
